topic: Build a 3D Animated Website Like Apple's in 30 Minutes Using AI
strengths: Extremely strong hook: 'Looks like Apple's. Built in 30 mins. With AI.' is a perfect pattern interrupt with a clear curiosity gap
High save factor: specific tool named (Claude Code), concrete workflow (drop product video → full site out), zero-to-hero narrative
Sharp contrast framing: Amateur = 3 weeks + 1 lakh + generic vs Pro = 30 mins + free — this is textbook share-worthy comparison
Punchy on-screen text: mostly 3-5 words per card, Hormozi-style bold caps, good pacing rhythm
Identity signal is strong: targets Indian professionals who feel ripped off by agencies — high 'this is me' resonance
Real numbers used: 1 lakh rupees, 3 weeks, 30 minutes — adds credibility and debate fuel
weaknesses: Avatar lighting is poor: heavy yellow/warm cast from below, dark background makes face muddy — loses perceived authority and production value

B-roll is mostly AI-generated stock (blender render, generic laptop shots, red-tinted paper) — feels disconnected from the actual build being described; no screen recording of the actual Claude Code workflow shown
No grand takeaway frame: the video ends with a follow CTA but lacks a single quotable screenshot-worthy closing line — 'Save this' is weak compared to what could be a punchy one-liner
Frame 5 B-roll has garbled/nonsensical text on screen (fake code list) — attentive viewers will notice this and it undermines credibility
Caption sync appears functional but the yellow highlight word styling is inconsistent — some frames highlight verbs, others highlight nouns, no clear emphasis logic
CTA is generic 'Follow @automatewithanand' — no specific next step like 'comment CLAUDE for the prompt' or 'link in bio for the template'
No actual result shown: the Apple-quality website is promised in the hook but never revealed in a full-screen showcase moment — this breaks the curiosity loop payoff
script_feedback: Hook is A-tier but the payoff is missing — you must show the final website in a full-screen reveal moment (frame 9-11 area) with a slow scroll or before/after split. The grand takeaway needs to be rewritten as a single sticky sentence, e.g., 'One prompt replaced a 1 lakh agency. Claude Code is the new web designer.' Add a specific engagement CTA: 'Comment WEBSITE and I'll send you the exact prompt.' The amateur vs pro framing is excellent but needs one more concrete data point — mention the exact prompt length or number of lines Claude generated to make it feel undeniably real.
visual_feedback: Avatar needs urgent lighting fix: add a key light at eye level and reduce the warm color temperature — current look feels amateur which contradicts the 'pro' positioning of the content. B-roll should be replaced with actual screen recordings of Claude Code terminal, the prompt being typed, and the website rendering live — this is the money shot and it's completely absent. The fake code/text in frame 5 B-roll is a credibility killer; use real terminal output or blur it. Final CTA frame (frame 12) has overlapping text ('SAVE THIS' bleeding into 'FOLLOW') — clean the z-layering. Consider adding a 2-second full-screen website reveal with a subtle zoom to deliver on the hook promise.

topic: 3 Levels of AI: Why You're Still the Bottleneck
strengths: Strong conceptual framework — Calculator vs Tool vs Employee vs Teammate progression creates a clear mental model that rewards rewatching
N8N template mention and agent-level explanation gives concrete save-worthy value
Level-based structure (Level 1, Level 2 implied) creates natural curiosity loop progression
Real use case teased — founder in Bengaluru, days it processed — adds specificity
CTA is present with follow prompt and save this callout
weaknesses: Hook is weak — opening frame shows generic hands on keyboard with Calculator/Tool/Employee text; no clear pattern interrupt or scroll-stopping visual tension
B-roll is generic stock footage throughout — hands typing, man at laptop, abstract network graphs — none custom or screen-recorded showing actual n8n workflows
Avatar energy is flat — low vocal energy implied by neutral expressions across all frames, minimal hand gestures, dim lighting with no key light on face
Grand takeaway frame is missing — no single screenshot-worthy quotable sentence displayed prominently; Save This frame is buried under follow CTA overlay
On-screen text inconsistency — some frames have 5+ words, font weight varies, bottom caption text competes with avatar space
Comment trigger is weak — no polarizing opinion, no direct question posed to viewer, Comment Agent label is confusing rather than debate-inducing
The Bengaluru founder story is teased but never lands — no rupees saved, no hours freed, no specific outcome shown
script_feedback: Hook needs complete rework: open with a specific outcome statement like 'A founder in Bengaluru replaced 3 employees with one n8n workflow — here is the exact template.' Current Calculator/Tool/Employee opening is too abstract and educational-feeling, not scroll-stopping. The level framework is good but needs concrete anchors at each level: Level 1 ChatGPT as calculator — you copy paste, Level 2 as tool — you prompt engineer, Level 3 as employee — it runs n8n workflows while you sleep. Grand takeaway must be one punchy sentence: 'Stop prompting. Start delegating. Build the agent.' The Bengaluru founder proof point needs real numbers: '47 leads processed in 3 days, zero manual input, ₹0 extra cost.' End CTA should say 'Comment AGENT and I will send you this n8n template' to drive both comments and saves.
visual_feedback: B-roll needs to be actual screen recordings of n8n workflows, not generic stock network visualizations — the current abstract node graphs look AI-generated and untrustworthy. Avatar lighting is too dim; add a key light at 45 degrees to create depth and energy. Eye contact is inconsistent — several frames show eyes looking off-camera. Caption style attempts Hormozi formatting but word highlighting is inconsistent (some orange, some red, some yellow with no clear hierarchy). The split-screen layout with B-roll top and avatar bottom is functional but the B-roll panel shows the same n8n template visual for 6+ consecutive frames with only color shifts, creating visual fatigue. Final CTA frame is cluttered — the FOLLOW overlay, @handle, tagline, and ONE PERSON COMMENT text all compete. Simplify to one action per frame.

topic: OpenAI Took the Pentagon Deal. Anthropic Didn't. Here's What That Means For You.
strengths: Clear 3-level framework creates structured info density that rewards rewatching
Level progression (Level 1/2/3) is a proven curiosity loop format that holds attention
Specific tool mention (n8n) and 'Bengaluru founder' case study adds save-worthy credibility
Hormozi-style captions with red highlight words are punchy and legible
CTA explicitly asks for comment ('Comment: Agent') and offers a template — strong save trigger
weaknesses: Hook (Frame 1) is generic — hand on keyboard + 'CALCULATOR' text is a weak pattern interrupt; doesn't stop the scroll for the target demo
B-roll is largely AI-generated stock imagery (man at laptop, cityscape silhouette) — feels templated and disconnected from actual speech content
Avatar lighting is flat and dim in every talking-head frame; low energy facial expression, minimal hand gestures visible, no direct eye contact intensity
Grand takeaway is split across frames 9-10 ('Not a tool. A teammate.') but never appears as a single bold screenshot-worthy frame — it's buried in the flow
The 'Bengaluru founder' trigger (Frame 8) uses an obviously AI-generated face which undercuts trust and relatability
No specific numbers anywhere — no rupees saved, no hours reclaimed, no metric that makes the share feel urgent
Level 2 explanation ('prompt it gives') is vague — viewer doesn't learn what changed or what to do differently
script_feedback: Hook needs a broader TAM opener — replace 'calculator' metaphor with something like 'Most of you are using ChatGPT like an intern. Here are 3 levels to make it your cofounder.' Level 2 is the weakest section: instead of 'prompt it gives,' show a concrete before/after (e.g., 'Level 2: You give it a system prompt. It writes 40 cold emails in your voice in 12 minutes.'). The Bengaluru founder trigger needs real specifics: 'A Bengaluru SaaS founder gave an AI agent access to his CRM. It wrote follow-ups, sent 87 emails, and closed ₹4.2L in pipeline — in one weekend.' Grand takeaway MUST be a single frame, single sentence, screenshot-bait: 'Stop prompting. Start delegating.' or 'AI isn't your tool. It's your first hire that works 24/7.' Currently it's split and diluted across two frames.
visual_feedback: B-roll quality is mid — the network/node graphics (Frames 3-6) are passable but the AI-generated people (Frames 8-9) look uncanny and hurt credibility. Replace with real screen recordings of n8n workflows or actual ChatGPT conversations. Avatar section needs urgent improvement: add a key light at 45 degrees, increase background separation (even a simple RGB strip), and coach for more animated delivery — current energy reads as monotone/flat. Caption text is well-formatted but the red highlight keyword selection is inconsistent (e.g., 'ON' highlighted in 'STUCK ON LEVEL' adds no emphasis value). Final CTA frame (Frame 12) is cluttered — 'SAVE THIS / FOLLOW / @automatewithanand / AI AUTOMATION' competes with the talking head below. Simplify to one action per frame. The 'LOT BIGGER' text at the very bottom is cut off and unreadable.

topic: Gemini 3.1 Just Commoditized Web Design — Here's What That Means For You
strengths: Touches on a genuinely anxiety-inducing topic (AI disrupting jobs) which triggers emotional engagement
Hormozi-style bold captions with keyword highlighting in orange are present and legible
Clear CTA end card with handle and follow prompt
Speaker maintains consistent eye contact and calm authority throughout
weaknesses: Hook is extremely weak — 'GOT DISRUPTED AND' is mid-sentence, gives no reason to stop scrolling, no pattern interrupt, no bold claim, no number, no face of shock
B-roll is generic stock (laptop screen, hand on keyboard, person at desk) with zero specificity — no actual AI tool shown, no before/after, no screen recording
No specific tool, framework, or concrete takeaway is named anywhere — nothing to save or bookmark
Grand takeaway frame is missing entirely; last substantive frame says 'THE SKILL ISN'T' which is incomplete and not screenshot-worthy
Pacing feels slow and lecture-like — no tempo change, no visual surprise, no pattern interrupt mid-video to sustain double-watch
Frame 7 is completely black — either a rendering error or dead air that kills retention
Subtitle fragments ('OF STUFF A', 'THAT SHOULD MAKE', '3.1 ACTUALLY') lack context in isolation, suggesting the script meanders without punchy standalone lines
No share trigger — no identity-based statement like 'Tag a developer who thinks they're safe' or relatable meme moment
script_feedback: The script needs a complete restructure. Hook (first 1.5s) must open with a specific, bold, falsifiable claim: e.g., 'A junior designer in Pune just lost a ₹8L/year job to one AI tool — here's which one.' The body needs exactly 3 triggers with real specifics: name the AI tool (e.g., Framer AI, v0.dev, Locofy), cite a real metric ('built a pixel-perfect landing page in 47 seconds'), and contrast old vs new ('what took a junior dev 3 days now takes 3 minutes'). The grand takeaway must be a single quotable sentence printed on screen for 2+ seconds: e.g., 'The skill isn't coding — it's knowing which AI to use and when.' The current script is vague, uses filler phrases like 'of stuff a' and '3.1 actually' without grounding them in anything concrete. Kill the meandering — every sentence must either shock, teach, or provoke.
visual_feedback: B-roll needs to be replaced entirely with screen recordings of actual AI tools generating websites, side-by-side comparisons, or at minimum high-quality relevant footage — not generic stock of hands on keyboards. The completely black Frame 7 is a critical error that will crater retention. Avatar lighting is flat and slightly underexposed on the left side; add a key light at 45 degrees. Speaker energy is too subdued for a disruption/urgency topic — needs more vocal variation, hand gestures, or lean-in moments. Caption text is functional but could benefit from larger font size and a subtle background blur behind text for better contrast. End CTA card is decent but should include a specific value promise ('Follow for weekly AI tool breakdowns') rather than just 'AI Automation · New Builds Every Week' which is vague.

topic: A junior designer just lost her forty-five thousand rupee job because Claude built a pixel-perfect website in thirty minutes
strengths: Clear comparison diagram in frame 5 showing typing 12+ hours vs Claude+Stitch deployed in 30 minutes is highly save-worthy and concrete
Captions are Hormozi-style with keyword highlighting in orange, readable and punchy at 3-5 words per line
Topic hits a real pain point — junior designers and front-end devs being disrupted by AI tools, which triggers identity-level engagement
CTA end card is clean with follow prompt and niche positioning (AI Automation, new builds every week)
weaknesses: Hook is weak — 'got disrupted and' is mid-sentence entry with no pattern interrupt, no shocking stat, no visual disruption to stop the scroll
Zero B-roll in the top half for 9 out of 12 frames — it is just a static talking head against a dark curtain with no visual variety, killing rewatch and retention
Lighting is flat and slightly underexposed on the left side of the face, making the avatar feel low-energy and amateur
Frame 9 shows a beautiful landscape image that has zero relevance to web design or AI automation — confusing B-roll choice
Grand takeaway is missing — no single quotable screenshot-worthy sentence; the video drifts from disruption to skill to funeral to website that sells without a clear punchline
No specific tool demo or screen recording shown — Claude and Stitch are mentioned in the diagram but never visually demonstrated
The word 'funeral' in frame 10 is attention-getting but feels hyperbolic without proof or emotional payoff
script_feedback: Hook must be rewritten: open with a concrete shock stat like 'A junior designer just lost her ₹45K/month job because Claude built a pixel-perfect landing page in 30 minutes.' Current hook drops viewers mid-thought with no open loop. The comparison diagram (frame 5) is the strongest moment — move it to the first 3 seconds as visual proof. Grand takeaway needs a single quotable line like 'The skill isn't designing the page — it's knowing what page to build and for whom.' Currently the video cycles through too many half-ideas (disruption, difference, pixel-perfect, funeral, skill) without landing one hard. Add a specific real example: name the website, show the before/after, mention the client or niche. 'Website that sells' on the CTA frame is generic — make it specific like 'I built a ₹2L landing page in 30 minutes using Claude + Stitch — here's how.'
visual_feedback: Avatar energy is low — eyes are slightly downcast, hands are fidgeting in a small range, and the dark curtain backdrop with flat lighting makes the frame feel like a 2021 Zoom call, not a 2025 creator reel. Lighting needs a key light from the right and a subtle rim light to separate from background. B-roll is critically missing — 9 of 12 frames are identical talking head shots with only caption changes. Need screen recordings of Claude generating code, Stitch deploying, the final landing page with scroll animations. The landscape photo in frame 9 is completely off-topic and damages credibility. Caption text is well-styled but the dark background sometimes makes the grey caption box blend — consider a slightly brighter or colored background bar. End CTA card is functional but the laptop image is dim and generic; replace with an actual screenshot of a deployed site.

topic: OpenAI Sided With the Pentagon. Claude Went #1 on the App Store.
strengths: Anthropic vs Pentagon standoff topic has genuine curiosity and controversy appeal
Captions are large, bold, Hormozi-style with highlighted keyword per line — readable on mobile
Multiple distinct topics (Anthropic/Pentagon, ChatGPT uninstalls, Claude downloads) create info density
CTA at end with follow graphic is clean and branded
weaknesses: Hook is generic — 'AI This Week' is a category label, not a pattern interrupt or open loop; no specific shocking stat or claim in frame 1
B-roll is low quality sketch-style diagrams (frames 2-3, 5-6) — feels like rough whiteboard notes, not polished infographics
Stock-looking AI-generated image of woman with laptop (frames 8-9) has no relevance to speaker or topic, breaks trust
No single grand takeaway frame — video ends on a follow CTA without a quotable screenshot-worthy line
Avatar lighting is flat and slightly underexposed with dark curtain background; low energy body language, minimal gestures
No specific tool, workflow, or actionable tip is given — video is news recap without a concrete save-worthy takeaway
Pacing feels like a listicle summary rather than a story arc; no tension build or payoff structure
script_feedback: Hook must be rewritten entirely — replace 'AI This Week' with the single most shocking stat upfront, e.g., 'The Pentagon just threatened to blacklist Anthropic — here's why that changes everything for you.' The Anthropic/Pentagon segment is the strongest story but needs stakes personalized to the viewer ('this affects every AI tool you use'). The ChatGPT uninstalls / Claude downloads segment needs hard numbers with source and timeframe. There is no grand takeaway — add a single punchy closing line like 'The AI cold war just started, and your favorite tools are the battlefield.' The CTA asks to comment 'stack' but gives no reason why — tie it to a specific deliverable or resource. Entire script lacks a 'why should I care' bridge for working professionals in India.
visual_feedback: Avatar needs better lighting — add a key light on the face; the dark curtain background is flat and low-production. Sketch diagrams in frames 2-3 and 5-6 should be replaced with clean, branded infographic cards with consistent fonts and colors. The AI-generated stock woman image in frames 8-9 is a trust killer — replace with actual app screenshots, data charts, or screen recordings. On-screen text overlays are the strongest visual element but the highlighted word choice is sometimes arbitrary (e.g., 'THE' highlighted in frame 4). End card with follow CTA is decent but the dimmed overlay makes the speaker almost invisible — either go full graphic or keep speaker well-lit. Overall production feels rushed; needs a consistent visual template across all B-roll segments.

topic: Your ChatGPT Memory Doesn't Have to Die When You Switch to Claude
strengths: Addresses a genuine pain point: ChatGPT context loss and starting over every session
Comparison table (Without Import vs With Import) is a bookmark-worthy visual with concrete benefits
Clear step-by-step structure hinted: open Claude.ai, go to settings, click add, run the prompt
Specific tools named: Claude.ai and ChatGPT, giving save-worthy actionable content
weaknesses: Hook is extremely weak — 'years of chat' with a plain talking head does not stop the scroll; no pattern interrupt, no shocking stat, no visual disruption
B-roll is essentially non-existent — dark slides with bold text and the same talking head; no screen recordings, no live demo of the import process
Avatar energy is flat throughout: low facial expression variance, muted lighting, no hand gesture variation, eyes frequently half-closed or looking away
On-screen captions are basic white-on-dark with one orange highlight word — not true Hormozi-style (no box, no animation, no size variation for emphasis)
Grand takeaway frame is missing entirely — video ends on a generic 'FOLLOW @automatewithanand' card with no quotable one-liner
The comparison table text is tiny and unreadable at mobile scroll speed; it needs to be broken into animated bullet reveals
No real numbers anywhere: no time saved, no before/after metric, no specific use case with rupees or hours
CTA is a cold 'follow me' rather than a warm tool-specific close like 'save this, open Claude tonight, paste the prompt from my bio'
Six Weeks / Forty Hours slide has no context at mobile speed — unclear what it refers to without audio
No face in frames 2, 5, 8, 9 — kills retention; algorithm penalizes faceless segments in talking-head format
script_feedback: Hook must be rewritten completely. Replace 'years of chat' with something like 'I imported 847 ChatGPT conversations into Claude in 90 seconds — here is what happened.' The comparison table content is strong but needs to be spoken as a dramatic before/after with specific numbers: 'Without import: 10 minutes wasted every single prompt. With import: zero setup, output in 8 seconds.' The 'six weeks / forty hours' slide is confusing without narration context — if it means training time saved, say it explicitly: 'That is 40 hours of context Claude now knows without you typing a word.' Grand takeaway is completely absent — end with a single quotable line like 'Stop training AI from scratch every morning. Import once, benefit forever.' CTA should be 'Save this reel. Link to the import prompt is in my bio. Do it tonight.' Add a relatable frustration trigger early: 'You know that feeling when ChatGPT forgets everything you told it yesterday?' to drive comments.
visual_feedback: Production quality is below threshold for virality. Lighting on avatar is dim and yellowish — needs a key light at 45 degrees and a subtle fill. Background dark curtain is flat and adds no visual interest; even a simple RGB strip or bookshelf would help. Comparison table needs to be full-screen with animated bullet point reveals, not a static tiny image in the top half. All instructional steps (open Claude, go to settings, click add) desperately need actual screen recording B-roll — the current dark placeholder slides with text kill retention and credibility. Captions need upgrade: add a dark background box, increase font size by 30%, use bold white with orange/yellow keyword highlights, and add subtle pop animation on each word. Avatar should be framed chest-up with more headroom removed. The follow CTA card at the end is generic — replace with a branded outro that includes the tool name and a save-trigger visual like a bookmark icon.

topic: Anthropic's Secret Design Tool Just Leaked as Open-Source — And It Costs Zero
strengths: Specific tool named — Open Design — gives a concrete, saveable takeaway
Strong niche hook: 'Anthropic uses internally' creates insider-knowledge curiosity
Multiple screen recordings of the actual tool/GitHub repo add credibility
Cost anchor of $1.20 per build is a specific, attention-grabbing number
CTA is explicit: comment 'Open Design' to get the link, driving engagement loop
Covers pain point well: Claude Design is rented/locked, this is open-source alternative
weaknesses: Hook frame 1 is visually flat — static talking head with dark curtain, no pattern interrupt or motion to stop scroll
On-screen text captions are inconsistent in word count — 'FEATURES RESPONSIVE MODERN' is 3 separate ideas crammed, loses punch
Avatar lighting is underexposed throughout; dark blue curtain blends with dark background making the presenter look low-energy
No single grand takeaway frame with a quotable one-liner — the message is spread across too many ideas
B-roll screen recordings are too small and unreadable at phone scale; viewer cannot actually see what Open Design looks like
Frame 7 'CLAUDE DESIGN: RENTED.' appears on blank black screen with no visual anchor — dead air moment
Final CTA frame has competing visual layers: follow overlay + 'in 6 months' caption + dimmed presenter — cluttered
No face zoom, no emoji, no color flash in first 0.5s — scroll-stop power is weak for Reels algorithm
script_feedback: Hook needs a broader TAM rewrite — instead of 'Anthropic uses internally' (only resonates with AI-deep audience), try 'Anthropic's secret design tool just leaked as open-source — and it costs $0.' This widens curiosity. The middle section jumps between too many selling points (export, restrictions, cost, model support) without a clear 3-act structure. Recommend: Act 1 — Problem (Claude Design is locked, $200/mo, no export). Act 2 — Solution (Open Design, free, self-hosted, works with Claude Code + Cursor + Gemini). The routing framework (Haiku→Classify, Sonnet→Draft, Opus→Reason) is the gold — lead with it earlier, not buried at frame 6. Add a concrete Indian-market example: 'One SaaS founder in Bangalore saved ₹3.2L/month by routing 70% of queries to Haiku'. End with a single quotable grand takeaway: 'Route by task complexity, not by habit — that one rule saves you 60% on AI costs.' Close with a warm CTA: 'Save this for your next Claude Code session.'
visual_feedback: Remove the black frame immediately — it reads as a glitch. Replace static text cards with screen recordings of Claude Code, Sakana Labs dashboard, or at minimum animated text builds. Avatar needs better lighting — add a key light from the front to eliminate the flat, dim look. Shoot slightly closer to increase energy and intimacy. Text overlays need consistent sizing: use max 4-5 bold words per line, ensure nothing is cut off ('EXPENSIV' truncation is unacceptable). Add a branded grand takeaway card at the end with the one-liner in large white text on dark background. Caption bar should be centered bottom-third with consistent bold-highlight style on every frame.
